ontology.foodon_gap_note: This item is a non-food disposable tableware set. FoodOn does not contain ontology terms for non-food consumer goods such as disposable bowls, plates, cups, spoons, and chopsticks. No defensible FoodOn code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 is a food classification system and does not cover non-food items such as disposable utensil sets. No applicable FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT does not contain a clinical concept for disposable tableware sets. No applicable code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One set comprising one disposable bowl (4 inch), one disposable plate (6 inch), one disposable cup (6 oz), one pair of disposable chopsticks, and one disposable rice-husk spoon; this is a non-food tableware item with no consumable serving size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: This field survey data listing (GD002822) is a 5-piece disposable tableware set sold by The Noodle Place at Circular Road, priced at SGD 1.00 per set. It is not a food item. The set is intended as supplementary party supplies for Chinese New Year (CNY) gatherings and events such as steamboat and yusheng celebrations. Each set contains: one 4-inch disposable bowl (次抛碗), one 6-inch disposable plate (次抛盘), one 6-oz disposable cup (次抛杯子), one pair of disposable chopsticks (一次性筷子), and one disposable rice-husk spoon (一次性勺子). The rice-husk spoon is notable as it is made from an eco-friendlier agricultural by-product material compared to conventional plastic. This item has no nutritional value, no caloric content, no macronutrients, and no glycemic impact. It is entirely inedible and should not be classified as a food product in any nutritional database. Its presence in a food delivery platform database is incidental to the restaurant's offering of supplementary non-food party supplies alongside food orders. Clinically, this item is irrelevant to any dietary condition including hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: This item is unambiguously a non-food disposable tableware set. The product description from field survey data is explicit and detailed. All nutrient values are confirmed zero by the nature of the item being inedible. No nutritional database lookup is required or applicable. Confidence is high in the classification and editorial determination.
notes: This record represents a non-food item — a 5-piece disposable tableware set — listed on field survey data. It is not a food or beverage and has no nutritional composition. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is an inedible tableware item containing no food substance. protein_g is naturally zero as this item contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as this item contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this item contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. sodium_mg is naturally zero. All micronutrient fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) are set to -1 as they are not applicable to a non-food item and are not reported in any food composition database. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per Veritas sentinel convention for non-food items with zero carbohydrate content. Multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per the Singaporean cuisine multilingual requirement, as this item is sold by a Singapore-based restaurant on a Singapore food delivery platform. Ontology codes are all null with match_type none as FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT do not contain terms for non-food disposable tableware. The food_group field is set to Non-food item as no standard Veritas food group applies.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is a precise canonical English description of the product. Local-language names are present in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean multilingual requirements. The item is correctly identified as a non-food tableware set.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are present as numeric values set to 0.0. Zero values are justified in notes with the phrase 'naturally zero' as this is an inedible non-food item. nutrient_source documents the editorial determination basis.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, and sugar_g are all present as numeric values (0.0). Zero values are justified as naturally zero for a non-food item.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to 1 (valid positive sentinel per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate non-food items). gi_category is low (GI 1 is <= 55). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source is documented. Convention is explained in notes and gi_source.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: All three ontology anchors (FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT) are null with match_type none. Gap notes are provided for each explaining that these ontology systems do not cover non-food disposable tableware items. Ontology honesty is maintained.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and explains what the item is, its cultural context (CNY, steamboat, yusheng), its components, material notes (rice husk spoon), its non-food nature, and its clinical irrelevance. common_user_queries and meal_context are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct, machine key type2_diabetes), and Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence appropriate to a non-food item.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list is comprehensive with English variants, Chinese script forms, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi entries, and romanized form. aliases_by_language is populated for all applicable languages. All strings in aliases_by_language are verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency check passed.

Iteration 2: Previous iteration produced invalid JSON (empty output). Rebuilt complete JSON from scratch.

Confirmed GI sentinel value of 1 with gi_category low. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (none). Confirmed multilingual names present for Singaporean context.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — This record correctly identifies GD002822 as a non-food disposable tableware set. All hard gates pass. All eight Q criteria pass. Zero macro values are justified with 'naturally zero' phrases. GI sentinel value of 1 is applied per convention. All -1 micronutrient fields are documented. Ontology is honest with match_type none and gap notes. Multilingual names cover English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean requirements. aliases_by_language and flat aliases are consistent. Score: 8/8.
